title: [FIRRTL][ExportVerilog] Better Underscore Wire Behavior
body:
The change introduce in https://github.com/llvm/circt/pull/2676 guarantees that all "named" Chisel things will be "tapped" in the output.  However, this can result in duplicate wires being emitted into the final Verilog design. 

Divine a way to improve the Verilog quality by, e.g., CSE'ing wires which are the same or by introducing a notion of symbols/don't touch which does not block optimizations, but will always exist in the output.

Consider the following:

```scala
circuit Foo:
  module Foo:
    input a: UInt<1>
    input b: UInt<1>
    output out: UInt<1>

    node x = or(a, b)
    out <= x
```

This produces the following Verilog (`firtool Foo.fir --strip-debug-info`):
```verilog
module Foo(
  input  a, b,
  output out);

  wire x;

  wire _x = a | b;
  assign x = _x;
  assign out = _x;
endmodule
```

Wires `_x` and `x` are the exact same. Here, `_x` should not exist and any uses should be replaced with `x`.
